Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 36 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 92 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 125 upvotes, 17 comments
  4. Building a new flash 97 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. My Favorite 39C3 Talks 32 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 226 upvotes, 58 comments
  7. The Rust calling convention we deserve (2024) 35 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. An interactive map of Flock Cams 412 upvotes, 136 comments
  9. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 180 upvotes, 54 comments
  10. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 96 upvotes, 26 comments
  11. “It turns out” (2010) 213 upvotes, 71 comments
  12. Google ends its 30 percent app store fee and welcomes third-party app stores 150 upvotes, 51 comments
  13. Approximation Game 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. It is sweet and fitting to die for one's country (1921) 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 423 upvotes, 205 comments
  16. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 799 upvotes, 448 comments
  17. The View from RSS 30 upvotes, 10 comments
  18. Glaze by Raycast 173 upvotes, 102 comments
  19. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 49 upvotes, 23 comments
  20. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 26 upvotes, 10 comments
  21. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 48 upvotes, 25 comments
  22. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 207 upvotes, 149 comments
  23. To understand our fascination with crystals, researchers gave some to chimps 81 upvotes, 51 comments
  24. Does that use a lot of energy? 113 upvotes, 76 comments
  25. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 26 upvotes, 12 comments
  26. MacBook Neo 1312 upvotes, 1667 comments
  27. Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral 141 upvotes, 164 comments
  28. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 12 upvotes, 8 comments
  29. Accessibility Issues Are Often Usability Issues 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
